g.data-narration: Welcome! In this lecture, we'll master the three fundamental operations on linked lists: Insert, Delete, and Display.
g.text: Insert, Delete, and Display Operations on Linked Lists

g.data-narration: Linked lists are dynamic data structures where nodes connect via pointers, enabling efficient insertion and deletion without array resizing.
g.text: A linked list is a linear data structure where elements (called
nodes) are stored in memory and connected through pointers.
Key Advantage: Dynamic size, efficient insert/delete O(1)

g.data-narration: Every node contains two parts: the data field storing information, and the next pointer pointing to the subsequent node.
Insert at Beginning: Create a new node, set its next to current head, then update head to point to the new node. Time: O(1)
g.text: Create new node → Point to current head → Update head

g.data-narration: Insert at End: Create new node with next=NULL, traverse to last node, then link last node to the new node. Time: O(n)
g.text: Create node → Traverse to last → Link last to new

g.data-narration: Insert at Position: Traverse to position-1, create new node, then link previous to new and new to next. Maintains order.
Delete from Beginning: Save current head, advance head to next node, then free the old head. Simplest deletion operation.
g.text: Save head → Move head to next → Free old head

g.data-narration: Delete from End: Traverse to second-last node, save last node, set second-last's next to NULL, then free last node.
g.text: Traverse to second-last → Set next to NULL → Free last

g.data-narration: Delete by Value: Search for node with value, if found at head delete from beginning, otherwise bridge previous to next and free node.
g.text: Search for value → Bridge gap → Free node

g.data-narration: Display Operation: Start from head, traverse while current is not NULL, print each node's data, then move to next node.
g.text: Start at head → Print data → Move to next until NULL

g.data-narration: Time Complexity: Insert/Delete at beginning O(1), all other operations O(n). Display is always O(n). Space is O(1).
Key Takeaways: Always validate NULL checks, free deleted nodes, and handle edge cases. Master pointer manipulation for success.
g.text: • Always check for NULL before dereferencing pointers
• Free deleted nodes to prevent memory leaks
• Handle edge cases: empty list, single node, invalid position
